hero.headline: The Earth’s Great Scar
hero.subHeadline: A corridor of alkaline lakes, ancient volcanic craters, and yellow-fever acacia forests.

theNyotaLens.architectNote: The Rift is the pulse of the country. We focus on walking safaris in yellow-fever acacia forests where you walk among giraffes in total silence.

sensoryChapters.narrativeBody: Boating among pods of hippos on Lake Naivasha as the eagles cry overhead. Water-based serenity.
Walking with giraffes on Crescent Island. No predators, just the quiet companionship of the tall ones.
The flamingo-lined shores of Lake Nakuru at dawn. A kaleidoscope of shifting pinks and silvers.

pairsWellWith.reasoning: The natural gateway to the Mara. A water-based interlude before the plains drama.
